name: Rutgers University Student Assembly (RUSA)
summary: The Rutgers University Student Assembly (RUSA) is the official governing body for undergraduate students at Rutgers, New Brunswick/Piscataway and allocates student fee funding to over 400 student organizations.

name: 90.3 The Core
summary: 90.3 The Core is the student run radio station on Livingston Campus. We work to deliver outstanding alternative radio programming with PHS, to embrace local music, and to engage with our community.

name: American Choral Directors Association
summary: The ACDA Chapter at Rutgers University aims to promote education and community in the Rutgers Choirs through professional developments, events, and exciting opportunities.

name: Anime and Japanese Environmental Society
summary: The purpose of our organization is to provide the students with a connection between Japanese animation, Japanese culture, and general entertainment.

name: SCREAM Theater
summary: SCREAM Theater is a peer educational, interactive theater program that provides information about interpersonal violence including sexual assault, dating violence, same-sex violence, stalking, bullying, and peer harassment.

name: Asian A cappella Group (Rutgers)
summary: Rutgers RAAG is an award-winning, co-ed South Asian fusion a cappella group based out of Rutgers University in New Brunswick, New Jersey.

name: Asian Student Council
summary: The ASC's vision is to excel as a dynamic presence in the Rutgers community, shaping and enriching the college experience of all Rutgers students. All are welcome, and encouraged, to participate as members.

name: Business Information Technology Society
summary: The purpose of the organization is to provide a network for Rutgers students with an interest in the Business Analytics and Information Technology major to increase opportunities and experiences in the field.

name: Cabaret Theatre
summary: Cabaret Theatre is a forum for students to learn about acting, directing, writing, business management, design, and construction through various workshops and hands-on experience.

name: Art History Student Association (Rutgers)
summary: The purpose of the association is to provide a network for students who are interested in art history or art, and to create a forum for students to discuss, debate and socialize over their love for the field.

name: Chi Alpha Epsilon
summary: The Epsilon Mu Chapter within the Rutgers University School of Arts and Science Educational Opportunity Fund (EOF) Program strives to enhance the academic achievements of all its present future members.

name: Cognitive Science Club
summary: Our organization is dedicated to creating an incredible Cognitive Science community here at Rutgers. We bring in top-tier speakers, thought-provoking debates, and mentorship opportunities!

name: American Cancer Society on RU Campus
summary: American Cancer Society on RU Campus is a nationwide collaboration of college students, faculty, and staff dedicated to eliminating cancer by initiating and supporting programs of the American Cancer Society in college communities.

name: Diabetes Link (Rutgers)
summary:
The purpose of the organization Rutgers Diabetes Link was founded for the
purpose of connecting college students with and affected by diabetes in the hopes that these
connections will create a support network.

name: College Avenue Players
summary: The College Avenue Players is an active performing arts organization based on the College Avenue campus of Rutgers University – New Brunswick.

name: Criminal Justice Organization
summary: We intend to inform and engage students, of relating majors, who are interested in the field Criminal Justice. To develop our members both academically as well as professionally, provide networking opportunities and engage them in community service.

name: Designer Genes: Rutgers Biotechnology Club
summary: Designer Genes is THE biotechnology club at Rutgers, dedicated to aiding in the professional development of students in the field, serving and educating the community, and encouraging discussion about biotechnology!

name: Dhol Effect (Rutgers University)
summary: Rutgers University Dhol Effect, also known as R.U.D.E., is the institution's only South-Asian fusion band composed of dynamic vocalists and instrumentalists.

name: Douglass Black Students' Congress
summary: "Our goal is to instill black consciousness into the college community"

name: Douglass D.I.V.A.S.
summary: This organization shall provide a network for students with a strong interest in women's issues, by providing a forum for students to discuss, debate, socialize and create lasting bonds.

name: Fashion Organization of Retail and Marketing
summary: FORM's goal is to unite Rutgers students with a passion for fashion, and provide them with opportunities to help them gain prospective careers in the business of fashion.

name: Fusion: The Rutgers Union of Mixed People
summary: Fusion: The Rutgers Union of Mixed People aims to unite people who identify with or are interested in the multiracial experience (multiracial, multiethnic, interracial relationships, transracial adoption, etc.).

name: Future Healthcare Administrators
summary: Student-led organization dedicated to developing future healthcare leaders.

name: Future Teachers Association
summary: The purpose of Future Teachers Association is to provide a network for students with a strong interest in teaching and/or education. This club provides helpful resources regarding graduate school applications, job opportunities, and scheduling.

name: Entrepreneurial Society (Rutgers)
summary: We are a community of innovators, creators and founders cultivating entrepreneurship and top talent at Rutgers University. Please check out our Instagram/discord page to keep up to date on upcoming events and opportunities!

name: Educational Opportunity Program Student Association
summary: The purpose of EOPSA shall be to serve as a liaison between the student body of the Educational Opportunity Fund Program and the staff.

name: GlobeMed at Rutgers University
summary: The mission of GlobeMed is to connect the assets of a student-led network to grassroots health organizations working in communities around the world.

name: Habitat for Humanity
summary: Rutgers Habitat for Humanity aims to advocate for our organization's vision to provide affordable and decent housing for families and individuals in need. We aim to promote the vision through fundraising and helping families through build sites.

name: Busch Student Center - Student Staff
summary: We provide services, activities, programs and facilities that complement the academic mission of the University.

name: Hindu Students Council (Rutgers)
summary: Rutgers HSC is a non-sectarian international forum for the education and promotion of the Hindu cultural and spiritual heritage.

name: Institute for Domestic and International Affairs (IDIA - Rutgers)
summary: IDIA-RU provides a forum for Rutgers students who wish to help high schoolers understand their role in global and domestic civil society. We organize 4 annual Model United Nations and Congress conferences to educate, engage, and empower these students.

name: Japanese Visual Culture Association
summary: The Japanese Visual Culture Association aims to spread and partake in Japanese visual culture by promoting appreciation and involvement in relevant activities.

name: Korean-American Scientists and Engineers Association
summary: KSEA-Rutgers serves as a university-based chapter for the nationally-established organization KSEA. We seek to provide members with networking opportunities as well as growth as scientists and engineers.

name: Film Productions (Rutgers University)
summary: Rutgers University Film Productions (RUFP) is a student-run organization that gives students of Rutgers University the opportunity to utilize and enhance their creative talents through the multi-faceted and exciting world of film production!

name: Little Investment Bankers of Rutgers
summary: LIBOR is Rutgers University’s student-run finance organization. Our purpose is to prepare students for careers in financial services. We focus on placing students in "front-office" positions at leading firms on Wall Street.

name: Latin American Womyn's Organization
summary: The Latin American Womxn’s Organization (LAWO) is a student-led cultural and advocacy group at Rutgers University dedicated to uplifting and empowering Latin American womxn.

name: LLEGO: The LGBTQQIA People of Color Organization at Rutgers
summary: LLEGO examines social, economic, political and educational issues based on ethnicity, sexual orientation and gender identity which affect the lives and well being of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, questioning, and intersex  people of color.

name: Meditation Club
summary: We aim to cultivate a community centered on learning and practicing various forms of meditation to achieve a fuller understanding of oneself. Together in conversation, we hold space for exploring all areas of life which the practice of meditation touches

name: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
summary: The purpose of this organization: to uplift and fight for the sole purpose of the minority, and to advance the social, political, economical, educational, and cultural status of the minority.

name: Mounted Patrol
summary: The purpose of this organization is to organize the Mounted Patrol division of the Community Service Officer program.

name: Muggle Mayhem
summary: The Rutgers Chapter of the Harry Potter Alliance hopes to utilize the zeal and interest regarding the Harry Potter universe to bring about change in the real world, while also connecting through games and activities at general meetings

name: oSTEM at Rutgers
summary: To provide services and support for LGBTQ students in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics at Rutgers

name: Rutgers Review (The)
summary: The Rutgers Review is an arts & culture magazine.

name: Venture Partners
summary: The Rutgers Venture Partner's mission is to equip the next generation of venture capitalists and entrepreneurs with the knowledge and network to shape the future of startups.

name: Pre-Optometry Professions Society
summary: RU-POPS is an organization that is dedicated to helping educate and inform its members about the field and profession of optometry. We help each and every student find their full potential and guide the way to becoming an eye doctor.

name: Oceanography Club
summary: The oceanography club provides a platform for students who are interested in oceanography, marine science, or just the ocean in general. It offers students a space to discuss and get involved with DMCS and learn about ocean science.

name: O Beta Alpha Kappa Omicron Nu
summary: Kappa Omicron Nu is a national honor society dedicated to recognizing and encouraging excellence in scholarship, research, and leadership in family and consumer sciences. The mission is to promote empowered leaders.

name: Q&A: Queer and Asian
summary: Q&A: Queer and Asian aims to be an open forum, safer space, and a place of community for Asian Pacific Islander and Desi American LGBTQA+ students through discussion and programming that promotes community building and social education.

name: Center for Youth Political Participation
summary: RU Voting is a nonpartisan effort to register, educate, and mobilize Rutgers students for elections; a project of the Darien Civic Engagement Project in conjunction with the Center for Youth Political Participation at Eagleton Institute of Politics.

name: Business Governing Association (Rutgers)
summary: The Rutgers Business Governing Association (RBGA) is the student governing body of the Rutgers Business School (RBS).

name: United Black Council
summary: The purpose of this umbrella organization of all African, African-American and Afro-Caribbean student organizations of the Rutgers, New Brunswick campus is to strengthen and unite the student organizations that represent the aforementioned communities.

name: Psychological Society
summary: The mission of Psychological Society is to provide an open forum where members have the opportunity to learn about psychology.

name: RU Makers
summary: In order to foster a community of people interested in DIY (Do It Yourself) projects including, but not limited to, electronics, 3D printing, laser cutting, and machine embroidery.

name: UNICEF (Rutgers)
summary: Join Rutgers UNICEF and work with people committed to making a difference through various interactive, philanthropic service events. We believe in putting children first around the world!

name: United Mission for Relief and Development
summary: The purpose of this club is to fulfill, on campus, the UMR purpose of striving “on a daily basis to implement targeted, cost-effective relief and development programs that improve lives".

name: Thaakat Foundation
summary: Our goal is to promote community service at Rutgers by participating in local projects while providing for our global projects. We aspire to bring hope to those outcasted in society and progress communities through education and healthcare development.

name: Unión Estudiantil Puertorriqueña (Rutgers)
summary: Rutgers Unión Estudantil Puertorriqueña [RUEP] is the ONLY Puerto Rican based organization at the collegiate level in New Jersey! RUEP was established in 1969 and we later helped co-establish the Department of Latino and Caribbean Studies.

name: TWESE, The Organization for African Students and Friends of Africa
summary: "The purpose of this student organization is to provide academic and cultural integration assistance of African students, friends of Africa and also to provide a medium for social and intellectual interaction"

name: Wanawake (Rutgers University)
summary: To serve as a breed of excellent men and women and leaders of integrity, vision, mission, and love, in lieu of the African history and experience. To unite college women for the interests of Africa and the promotion of its cultures.

name: Pilot Me (RU)
summary: Pilot Me Mentoring is a community service oriented organization that actively enriches the youth of the surrounding community of Rutgers University in New Brunswick.

name: Reach Out And Read
summary: Rutgers Reach Out and Read is a nonprofit organization that gives young children in New Brunswick a foundation for success by incorporating books into pediatric care and encouraging families to read aloud together.

name: Scarlet Cross - The Rutgers Medieval History and Reenactment Club
summary: Scarlet Cross Medieval Reenactment is an undergraduate student organization at Rutgers University in New Brunswick, New Jersey. Our group's purpose is to study and re-enact some of the more interesting parts of Medieval history.

name: Verbal Mayhem Poetry Collective
summary: Verbal Mayhem’s mission is to provide local and national opportunities for poetic performance, artistic growth and competition. Please join our Facebook group for updates https://www.facebook.com/groups/VerbalMayhem/

name: Speech and Hearing Club, a NSSLHA Chapter (Rutgers University)
summary:
For a full description of our club, click on our page here!

Please join our Facebook group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ruspeechandhearing
and our Instagram Page @ rutgersnsslha

name: Rutgers Commuter Student Association
summary: The Rutgers Commuter Student Association is an inclusive volunteer student organization whose primary role is to serve commuter students’ needs and interests while building a community on campus.

name: Roll for Initiative: the Rutgers Dungeons & Dragons Club
summary: This club’s purpose is to grant students who play Dungeons & Dragons and other tabletop roleplaying games a place to meet one another, to share resources such as maps, rulebooks, and figurines, and to organize games with fellow club members.

name: Pakistani Student Association
summary: To inform all interested persons of the culture and traditions of Pakistani culture.

name: SEBS Governing Council
summary: The SEBS Governing Council (SGC) serves the student body of the Rutgers School of Environmental and Biological Sciences (SEBS).

name: Unplugged - The Board Gaming Club
summary: Do you like board games? If so, consider joining us to play some board games and make new friends! Whether you’re new to the hobby or are a seasoned board gamer, everyone is welcome!

name: Supply Chain Association (Rutgers University)
summary: RUSCA is the premier supply chain management club on the New Brunswick campus. Our organization focuses on connecting supply chain students with corporate and academic experiences to promote their growth as future Supply Chain professionals.

name: Undergraduate Mathematics Association
summary: The purpose of the organization will be to support and encourage the study of undergraduate mathematics by fostering professional growth, development and interest in the mathematical sciences among the students of Rutgers University.

name: Undergraduate Student Alliance of Computer Scientists
summary: USACS is Rutgers' main Computer Science student org! Our mission is to support CS students academically, socially, and professionally. We accomplish this via our 4 committees: Technology, Education, Community, and Outreach.

name: Landscape Architecture Club (Rutgers Student Chapter of the ASLA)
summary: Join us on an exploration of the many facets of Landscape Architecture. This club expands our education with professional events, collaborations with other clubs, and community involvement. All are welcome, for LA only benefits from diverse disciplines!

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
summary: To educate its members about the field of public relations through workshops, panels and speakers. The chapter's mission is to enhance members' knowledge of public relations by providing access to professional development opportunities.

name: Black Mens Collective (The)
summary: To aid in the development of male leaders of color, strive for academic excellence, and provide mentorship opportunities.

name: Chinese Student Organization
summary: To raise awareness and help establish identity by providing students with the opportunity to learn and socialize with others through common grounds in Chinese and Asian American culture.

name: Desi Intercultural Youth Association
summary: To celebrate various South Asian Cultures within our student body and to spread awareness of both South Asian Cultural norms and activities, as well as awareness of issues affecting the South Asian community within Rutgers University.

name: Douglass Student Recruitment Network
summary: The purpose of DSRN is to provide prospective and enrolled students with the opportunity to explore and understand Douglass Residential College through the Douglass experience.

name: Kidney Disease Screening and Awareness Program (Rutgers)
summary: The Kidney Disease Screening and Awareness Program is a student-run organization that provides free kidney health screenings and health education to underserved communities.

name: Building Research, Advocacy, and Innovation in Neuroscience
summary: BRAIN strives to inform its members and the Rutgers community, of global developments and interests in the fields of Neuroscience and Psychology through biweekly meetings, museum trips, volunteer work, hosting guest speakers, charity events, and more!

name: Mason Gross Student Governing Association
summary: Our mission is to enhance education and opportunity for Mason Gross students; to promote the arts, including an open forum for interdisciplinary collaboration and communication; and to create a strong voice for the student body internally and externally.

name: Alpha Delta Phi
summary: Since its creation in 1832 at Hamilton College, Alpha Delta Phi has held itself to the highest standard of excellence.  Our 50,000+ alumni include former Presidents and Senators of the United States, as well as leaders in business and the sciences.

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
summary: Consistent with our Founders' thoughts in 1898, our primary purpose at Rutgers is to create an environment for sisters in which lasting friendships and our vows of sisterhood emanate all aspects of our Fraternity.

name: Chi Psi
summary: Chi Psi at Rutgers University is one of 32 Alphas (or Chapters) of the first social Fraternity, founded on May 20th, 1841. Our history, and devotion to brotherhood, defines our experience as Chi Psis.

name: Delta Gamma
summary: Consistent with our Founders' thoughts in 1873, our primary purpose is to create an environment for our members in which lasting friendships and our vows of sisterhood emanate all aspects of our Fraternity.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
summary: America's Foremost Co-Ed Business Fraternity

name: Sigma Phi Delta
summary: the organization is the only fraternity of its kind that draws its membership exclusively from male engineering students at ABET-accredited colleges and universities

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
summary: SigEp is redefining fraternity on college campuses across the country.

name: RUSA Allocations Board
summary: The RUSA Allocations Board is an affiliated committee of RUSA and is responsible for the allocation of funds to registered Rutgers University Student Organizations as well as various registered events on an as needed basis.

name: Iota Phi Theta
summary: As Iota Phi Theta continues to grow and strengthen, so will its commitment to make meaningful contributions to society in general, with particular emphasis in the African-American community

name: Kappa Sigma Fraternity
summary: Kappa Sigma is focused upon the Four Pillars of Fellowship, Leadership, Scholarship and Service.

name: Nu Alpha Phi
summary: Asian-Interest social fraternity that seeks to build a bond of brotherhood passed down from generation to generation that focuses on self-development, as well as creating a positive impact in the community through service and outreach.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha
summary: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc., develops leaders, promotes brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing service and advocacy for our communities.

name: Pi Kappa Phi
summary: Pi Kappa Phi: Iota Tau Chapter at Rutgers University

name: Professional Development Committee (Student Affairs)
summary: Professional Development Committee for Division of Student Affairs.  This committee provides professional development for professional full time staff members employed in the Division of Student Affairs.

name: Rutgers University Programming Association
summary: The Rutgers University Programming Association (RUPA) is the university-wide student programming council that serves the entire Rutgers community.

name: RU-TV Broadcast Communication Living-Learning Community: Busch Campus
summary: This community offers students the opportunity to work and learn in the field of broadcast communication while living with students who share common interests.

name: Livingston Social Justice and Service Learning Community (IMPACT): Livingston Campus
summary: The overarching goal of IMPACT is to produce the next generation of social justice advocates, activists, and educators through credit-bearing, co-curricular, and community service programs.

name: Residence Hall Association (RHA)
summary: The Rutgers Residence Hall Association (RHA) is a student government organization which represents all students living in the residence halls across all five campuses.

name: Demarest Residential Community: College Ave Campus
summary: The Demarest community offers students the ability to participate in at least one of ten special interest sections.  Sections are small groups of students that come together based on a common interest e.g. Performing Arts, Music Appreciation, etc.

name: Latin Images: College Ave Campus
summary: The Latin Images Special Interest Section was founded in 1977 as a result of efforts by the Latino community at Rutgers College in an effort to provide a place for students to call their “Home away from Home.”

name: Alpha Pi Mu
summary:
The Mission of Alpha Pi Mu
 •To confer recognition upon the industrial engineering student who has shown exceptional academic interests and abilities in his field.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
summary: AIChE is the world's leading organization for chemical engineering professionals, with over 45,000 members from over 100 countries.

name: Engineering Governing Council
summary: The Engineering Governing Council (EGC) is the governing body of the Rutgers University School of Engineering on the New Brunswick/Piscataway Campus.

name: Chi Epsilon
summary: We are members of the Rutgers University chapter of Chi Epsilon - the national civil engineering honor society.

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers
summary: IISE is the world's largest professional society dedicated solely to the support of the industrial engineering profession and individuals involved with improving quality and productivity.

name: Minority Engineering Educational Task
summary: MEET is a premier organization on campus that promotes the increase of the minority retention within engineering. The organization works to increase the professionalism of members while also insuring that they are academically sound.

name: Omega Chi Epsilon
summary: Omega Chi Epsilon is the National Honor Society for Chemical Engineering undergraduate and graduate students.

name: Pi Tau Sigma
summary: The official Rutgers - New Brunswick Iota chapter of Pi Tau Sigma, the International Mechanical Engineering Honor Society. Eligible members will have the chance to be inducted their junior or senior year.

name: Rutgers International Society for Optics and Photonics Student Chapter
summary:
SPIE is the International Society for Optics and Photonics, a highly renowned society with more than 17,000 members worldwide.
The society is related to the field of photonics, which is the science of light and a field with contributions and applications

name: Rutgers Solar Car Team
summary: Rutgers Solar Car Team strives to provide ambitious students of all majors an opportunity to gain hands-on experience working on a project that requires dedication, teamwork, and ingenuity, in order to produce a competitive and roadworthy solar vehicle

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: The Rutgers SWE Section consists of student of all genders whose cause is to support and promote women in engineering. SWE provides a platform for women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders and demonstrates the value of diversity

name: Rutgers Residence Life
summary: Rutgers Residence Life supports the mission of the University by fostering the creation of safe, comfortable, and inclusive residential communities which are conducive to student development.

name: Living-Learning Community Peer Mentors
summary: The Peer Mentors for Living Learning Communities will be primarily responsible for the academic and professional development of their respective residents.

name: Scarlet Honor Council
summary: The Scarlet Honor Council is a team of students committed to upholding Rutgers' community standards and promoting a safe and civil campus environment.

name: Society of Plastics Engineers
summary: The Rutgers Student Chapter of SPE focuses on introducing students to the world of plastics and connecting them with professionals in the industry. Students from any major with an interest in plastics and their applications are welcome to join.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
summary: With a full suite of programs and resources to help you thrive, ASCE membership will help provide you with the knowledge and opportunities you’ll need to jump into the industry, get on the right career path, and have fun doing it.

name: MEDLIFE - Rutgers University
summary: MEDLIFE Rutgers supports the mission to provide healthcare, education, and safe housing to low-income families globally. It raises awareness, fundraises, and engages students in service, promoting a collaborative, inclusive, and responsible culture.

name: Rutgers Personal Finance Club
summary: To empower undergraduate students toward achieving financial independence, flexibility, and stability and provide them with opportunities to develop a career in portfolio and investment management.

name: Chemistry Society (Rutgers)
summary: The Rutgers Chemistry Club aims to foster a passion for chemistry across all majors by organizing interactive hands-on events, accessible science seminars, social gatherings, and volunteering opportunities, welcoming students from any discipline.

name: Douglass Friends of UNFPA
summary: The Reproductive Health Universal TaskForce (formerly The Douglass Friends of UNFPA) is for students who are interested in population and development issues, reproductive and maternal health, and infant mortality.

name: Phi Sigma Tau, National Honor Society in Philosophy
summary: The purpose of Phi Sigma Tau is to encourage interest and activity among students and to promote ties between philosophy departments in accredited institutions.

name: Engineers in Action
summary: Engineers in Action's Rutgers Chapter aims to engage students in order to help provide resources and opportunities to use their academic training to provide essential infrastructure for villagers suffering from rural isolation.

name: Rutgers New Brunswick SACNAS (Advancing Chicanos/Hispanics & Native Americans in Science)
summary: The Society for the Advancement of Chicanos and Native Americans in Science is an organization meant to foster the success of underrepresented minorities in science. SACNAS serves as a support group that provides all members with the tools to succeed.

name: Center for Latino Arts and Culture
summary: The Center for Latino Arts and Culture is a student-centered, inclusive community where students can better integrate experiential, academic, and service-learning about Latine/x arts and culture at Rutgers.

name: Her Campus Rutgers
summary: Want to join a community of empowered and creative women? Join Her Campus for the ideal place to share your voice through article writing and digital content creation! Our fun-filled social events are the perfect opportunity to find a family at Rutgers.

name: Hindi-Urdu Language and Culture Club
summary: The Hindi-Urdu Language and Culture Association is a space for students interested in the Hindi and Urdu languages in which they can explore the Hindi-Urdu language, its literature, and the culture of its speakers.

name: International Friendship Program
summary: The International Friendship Program (IFP) is about bringing Rutgers international students together with Rutgers and local community members in a comfortable environment that is conducive to cross-cultural friendship and learning.

name: Rutgers Geek Week
summary: Geek Week is an nationally recognized annual week of events celebrating all things “geeky” at Rutgers University.

name: Sigma Tau Delta
summary: Our local chapter of Sigma Tau Delta, the International English Honor Society, aims to promote exemplary scholarship and literary engagement at Rutgers University.

name: Art and Design Club (Rutgers)
summary: Join and pursue all forms of art and design including drawing, painting, crafts, and more! We meet Fridays 5:30-7PM at Murray Hall.

name: Rutgers Packaging Engineering Club
summary: Rutgers Packaging Engineering Club allows for students majoring in packaging engineering or completing a packaging certificate to connect and network with fellow students, company representatives, and alumni.

name: Gift of Life - Rutgers Chapter
summary: Gift of Life – Rutgers Chapter raises awareness for the bone marrow donation process. Gift of Life - Rutgers Chapter collaborates with the Gift of Life national organization to help raise awareness and get everyone swabbed into the bone marrow registry.

name: RU Origami Club
summary: Join our club to learn new origami folds every week, make new friends, and participate in service events to give back to the community! Snacks, drinks, and paper is always provided. New members and beginners are always welcome!

name: Accounting Association (Rutgers University)
summary: The RUAA, Rutgers University Accounting Association, strives to provide current, new, and prospective accounting students with the resources they need to improve themselves and succeed both at Rutgers and in the professional world.

name: RU Progressive
summary: RU Progressive organizes to bring about progressive change in all spheres of our community through civic education, political action, voter registration, etc.

name: SGC Allocations Board
summary: The SEBS Governing Council Allocations Committee allocates funding from SEBS student fees to student organizations within the School of Environmental and Biological Sciences.

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
summary: NRHH is the leadership based honorary comprised of exemplary residential students who value service and recognition.

name: First Generation Student Union
summary: FGSU provides a community of support and resources for academic and professional success. Students will discuss key issues affecting first generation students, attend workshops, meet speakers and scholars, and volunteer for community service trips.

name: Barbara Voorhees Mentors
summary: The Barbara Voorhees Mentor Program, located at the heart of the Douglass mission, serves to connect incoming students with the Douglass Residential College. Mentors are assigned to individual sections of the first-year course "Issues in Women’s Leadersh

summary: This Organization is sponsored by the Douglass Residential College Program and is advised by Kimberly Apadula, kapadula@echo.rutgers.edu

name: STEM Research Advisory Board

name: The Daily Targum
summary: Founded in 1869, the Daily Targum is the second oldest collegiate newspaper in the country. Student-written, student managed, and independent since 1980; the Daily Targum is dedicated to providing news coverage to the Rutgers community.

name: Chem-E-Car
summary: Sponsored by the American Institute of Chemical Engineers, or AIChE, the Chem-E-Car competition is a bi-annual event which tests the rigor, teamwork and creativity of individual AIChE chapters.

name: F.A.C.E Modeling Team
summary: A unique modeling team that promotes fashion, art, confidence and entertainment. Our purpose is to combine the love of fashion and modeling techniques to exude and build confidence.

name: National Black Law Students Association-Pre Law Division
summary: The mission of NBLSA is to increase the number of culturally responsible Black and minority attorneys who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

name: Pre PA Club (Rutgers)
summary: This club is meant to guide students that are interested in the physician assistant profession, touching on everything from how to be a competitive applicant to the daily life of a PA!

name: Petey Greene (Rutgers)
summary: Petey Greene is a national program that enables college students to volunteer in free, high-quality tutoring to incarcerated and formerly incarcerated people while educating volunteers on the injustice in our carceral system. Feel free to contact us!

name: The Space Technology Association of Rutgers
summary: A SEDS chapter uniting space enthusiast of all majors! Our projects include a CubeSatellite, High Altitude Ballooning, and Lunabotics team.

name: Afro - Latinx Student Organization
summary: This Student Organization provides a voice to those who identify with the Afro-Latinx Community at Rutgers University. This organization's goal is to educate and promote awareness of the Afro-Latinx Community through events, programs, and meetings.

name: Office for Violence Prevention and Victim Assistance (Rutgers University)
summary: VPVA is committed to creating a community free from violence through direct service, education, training & policy development.

name: Asian American Cultural Center
summary: The Asian American Cultural Center offers a myriad of social, cultural, and educational opportunities designed to promote, respect and embrace the unique histories and cultural identities within the Asian Pacific Islander Desi Arab (APIDA) diaspora.

name: Paul Robeson Cultural Center
summary: The Paul Robeson Cultural Center offers programs, initiatives, and services that reflect the robust history, heritage, and diversity of the African diaspora.

name: Center for Social Justice Education and LGBT Communities (Rutgers University)
summary: The Center for Social Justice Education and LGBT Communities (SJE) provides educational and social programs, leadership development, and policy consultation for LGBTQIA and ally students, faculty, staff, and community members.

name: Student Affairs Marketing, Communications, and Innovative Technology (Rutgers University)
summary: the Division of Student Affairs creates innovative learning opportunities, programs, and services that prepare students to lead, serve, and become engaged members of a global society.

name: Off-Campus Living and Community Initiatives
summary: Rutgers University Off-Campus Living and Community Initiatives is here to help students interested in living off campus by providing online resources and educational programming.

name: Security Club (Rutgers)
summary: We are an organization dedicated to training Rutgers students to become ethical hackers and cybersecurity analysts. We compete against other universities in cybersecurity competitions such as CCDC, HackTheBox, and other international CTFs.

name: Institute for Women's Leadership (Rutgers University)
summary: The Institute for Women's Leadership is a consortium of teaching, research, and public service units of Rutgers -New Brunswick.

name: Rhythm Games Club
summary: The Rhythm Games Club exists to unite fans of rhythm games together and provide an opportunity to discuss rhythm games, go out to arcades, watch high-level gameplay, or hang out with friends.

name: Entomology Club (Rutgers)
summary: Entomology Club is a place for insect enthusiasts and undergraduate students studying entomology to meet and form a vibrant community around a love of terrestrial arthropods.

name: Alzheimer's Buddies (Rutgers)
summary: Our mission is to improve the lives of dementia patients by pairing Rutgers students with dementia patients for weekly 1-on-1 visits. Volunteers will develop a strong friendship with their buddy and learn about the person underneath the disease.

name: Amnesty International (Rutgers)
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be the promotion of human rights by providing a forum for activism and for students.

name: Business School Blockchain Hub (Rutgers)
summary: RBS Blockchain Hub is the Official Cryptocurrency and Blockchain Organization of Rutgers. Our mission is to educate the Rutgers community on the emerging technology blockchain and its cryptocurrency applications.

name: RUcketlist
summary: The RUcketlist is a number of experiences or achievements that a person hopes to have or accomplish during their time at Rutgers. Start your journey at Rutgers and check off the items on your RUcketlist!

name: Residence Life: College Ave Campus
summary: Group used to facilitate communication throughout the college ave campus.

name: Rutgers University Jains
summary: “RU Jain” is an organization that seeks to connect Jains across campus and other like-minded individuals through the shared values and ideals Jains from all walks of life hold. Membership is open to all students!

name: Mind and Media
summary: Mind in Media is an organization that helps students learn about film in a psychiatry lens. Members of the club are encouraged to contribute to a weekly blog and as well as participate in creating their own collaborative film.

name: Nu Rho Psi
summary: Nu Rho Psi was formed to encourage careers in brain science and prepare students for induction into the Nu Rho Psi national honor society. This club is directly affiliated with the Nu Rho Psi national honor society chapter at Rutgers University

name: SAS Honors Program (Rutgers University Department)
summary: The SAS Honors Program encourages selected creative, talented, and motivated students in SAS and RBS to take advantage of all that Rutgers, a major public research university, has to offer, while fostering a strong sense of community and leadership.

name: SAS Transfer Center
summary: The Transfer Center assists transfer students new to Rutgers University and current students interested in taking courses at other institutions.

name: Academic Programs at Douglass Residential College - DRC
summary: Academic Programs provides innovative programming for Douglass women through the Douglass Course, Global Village, Gender and Arts, and more.

name: Communications Department at Douglass Residential College - DRC
summary: We are an office of professional staff, graduate assistants and undergraduate students with a focus on providing seamless communications processes, consistent updates through multiple media platforms and maintaining a cohesive brand.

name: Douglass Residential College Community Engagement
summary: Douglass Community Engagement empowers students to collaborate with the community, build equitable, sustainable relationships, and become change agents for some of the most pressing issues of our time.

name: Enrollment, Scholarship, Housing and Commuter Engagement at Douglass Residential College at Douglass Residential College - DRC
summary: We are an office of three professional staff with a focus on recruiting a new incoming class each year, awarding any available scholarships and assisting with Douglass housing for Douglass students.

name: Traditional Events at Douglass Residential College - DRC
summary: Through traditions, we create new memories, make new friends, and remind ourselves of our larger stories. We keep two major traditions at Douglass Residential College: Yule Log and Sacred Path.

name: Alumnae Engagement at Douglass Residential College - DRC
summary: As alumnae, you can make an impact in the lives of current Douglass students through volunteer opportunities this spring.

name: Eagleton Institute of Politics
summary: The Eagleton Institute of Politics explores state and national politics through research, education, and public service, linking the study of politics with its day-to-day practice.

name: Rutgers University School of Engineering
summary: Rutgers School of Engineering is a place of possibilities—for students seeking success, for researchers seeking to create new technologies, and for faculty members and industry leaders seeking to form powerful and productive partnerships.

name: Herpetology Program of Rutgers (The)
summary: Our goal is to help educate the public about the care and management of reptiles as pets, but also in a broader spectrum with conversation and restoration of reptilian species.

name: NABA, Inc. Rutgers New Brunswick Chapter” (NABA)
summary: The purpose of NABA is to provide a network for Black students with a strong interest in business. NABA seeks to bring together underrepresented business students from the past, present, and future to enrich and support each other.

summary: The Rutgers Engineering Honors Council (REHC) is composed of student representatives to support excellence in the engineering community through a myriad of outreach, social, industry mentorship, and keynote speaker events.

name: J Voorhees Zimmerli Art Museum
summary: The Zimmerli Art Museum at Rutgers University is one of the largest and most distinguished university-based museums in the country.

summary: TEACH is an international nonprofit organization that brings exciting, hands-on, educational programming to children in hospitals through visits, science kit drop-offs, virtual experiment demos, and other events.

name: Blueprint
summary: Blueprint is a community of students passionate about building better products. We are a close-knit group of product managers, designers, and engineers working together to launch, build, and improve products and break into product careers.

name: Rutgers Transfer Student Association
summary: To provide a network for students who have transferred into Rutgers University to utilize and give transfer students an equal opportunity to form bonds with other transfers and receive aid and mentoring from more senior transfers.

name: Sister2Sister (Rutgers University)
summary: RU Sister2Sister is a community service-based organization that seeks to spread breast cancer awareness through education, advocacy, and programming on all Rutgers University campuses alongside our mother chapter, Sister2Sister Inc.

summary: THRIVE Student Support Services is a federally funded program designed to increase the retention and graduation rates of first-generation and low-income college students.

summary: Diversity Peer Educators (DPEs) consist of Rutgers–New Brunswick students committed to educating their peers on topics of diversity, inclusion, and social justice.

summary: The Office of Veteran and Military Programs and Services (OVMPS) at Rutgers University works to provide a supportive environment for our military affiliated community

summary: RU e-NABLE is a local chapter of a global group of volunteers who modify and 3D print prosthetic upper limb devices to give to children and adults in need for free!

summary: The Rutgers University Student Activities Business Office (SABO) administers and manages the financial resources of the undergraduate student groups.

summary: This club will create an inclusive and supportive community of LGBTQ+ individuals and allies pursuing degrees in tech and relevant industries and mentor LGBTQ+ students by providing educational, mentorship, and leadership opportunities.

Our activities are designed to foster the interests of students in the graduate program in Bilingualism and Second Language Acquisition at the Dept. of Spanish and Portuguese. 
Graduate Student Lounge with coffee station available at ABW 5192.

summary: Rutgers Seeding Labs focuses on empowering scientists in developing countries to enrich the global research community. Talent is everywhere, resources are not.

summary: Knights for Recovery is a collegiate student group supporting students and individuals in recovery. We provide a safe, inclusive space for students in recovery and allies, to acknowledge and support their unique challenges. All are welcome to join!

summary: Our aim is to empower those who share NCNW's values, advancing African American women & social justice at Rutgers through education & advocacy. We foster inclusive leadership and initiatives, highlighting diverse voices & advocating for systemic change.

about the mission-critical industry and data centers.

summary: Rutgers Anti-Caste Collective (Formerly FABPPSSWA) is a graduate student organization at Rutgers University dedicated to raising awareness about caste-based oppression and its intersections with other social injustices.

name: Bloustein Social Justice Committee
summary: The Bloustein Social Justice Committee (BSJC) provides an independent space for students to embody, integrate, and act upon principles of social justice within the Edward J. Bloustein School of Planning and Public Policy, Rutgers University.

name: Graduate Economics Student Association
summary: The mission of GESA is to foster a welcoming environment that supports both the academic success and social engagement of graduate economics students. We strive to create a “family” where all members feel they belong.

name: Saudi Student Association
summary: The Saudi Student Association (SSA) connects and supports Saudi students at Rutgers, fostering cultural exchange and promoting Saudi traditions through events, programs, and gatherings.

name: Birdwatching Association (Rutgers University)
summary: RUBA strives to host exciting birding trips, provide opportunities to help with conservation efforts, learn about careers with birds, and more avian antics. It's a community for anyone who appreciates wild birds!

name: Hacky Sack Club (Rutgers) (Provisional Organization 24/25)
summary: A casual and lackadaisical club for socializing and hacky-sacking. Little to no commitment is our catchphrase. This club is simply meant to facilitate people's hacky-sacking desires while remaining low pressure and being recreational.

name: Being Black and Queer
summary: Being Black and Queer hopes to create a safe space for people who identify as both Black and LGBTQIA+ at Rutgers University in New Brunswick. We are a pillar of education, breaking boundaries and stereotypes to better unite and amplify our community.

name: Arab/Islamic Cinematography Club (Provisional Organization 24/25)
summary: The Arab/Islamic Cinematography Club at Rutgers University aims to educate and engage students through the exploration of Arab and Islamic history, culture, and civilization via the medium of cinema.

name: Graduate Society of Healthcare Leaders
summary: We are a student run organization that helps promote and run events within the Masters in Healthcare Administration program. Our goal is to shine light on events that are taking place that could be beneficial to student that attend them.

name: Kurdish Studies (Graduate Student Association)
summary: Kurdish Studies Graduate Student Association organizes academic and cultural events concerning the kurds and Kurdistan.

name: Mason Gross Art and Design (Mason Gross Graduate Student Association)
summary: The mission of the MGGSA is to further the education and professional advancement of Mason Gross graduate students outside of the current program curricula.

name: Library and Information Science Student Association (Graduate Organization)
summary: The Library and Information Science Student Association is the Rutgers University graduate student chapter of the American Library Association. Rutgers MI students are automatically members of LISSA and can choose their degree of involvement.

name: Rutgers University Synapse
summary: Synapse is dedicated to supporting individuals with brain injuries by cultivating a strong social support network, raising public awareness, and inspiring the next generation of professionals in brain injury care

name: Women in Data Science
summary: WiDs Rutgers was founded in 2023 and aims to uplift all students interested in or intending to pursue a career in Data Science.

name: Rutgers Enlightened Pages
summary: The Rutgers Enlightened Pages fosters critical thinking through weekly discussions of Kafka, Nietzsche, and Dostoevsky. With spirited debates and bi-monthly trips to NJ’s historic and political sites, members engage deeply with today’s defining questions

name: The Offline Project
summary: Helping students reduce their social media usage and connect in-person!

name: RU HART
summary: RU HART is Rutgers’ Human Factors and Ergonomics Society Student Chapter, fostering student learning and collaboration in designing systems, products, and environments that enhance human performance and well-being.
